title: Anthropic says US lifts export ban on its advanced AI tools
description: Fable and Mythos were abruptly suspended in June over concerns that they could be used by hackers.
content:
The US government has lifted an export ban on Anthropic's most advanced artificial intelligence (AI) tools, just weeks after ordering it to restrict access to them over national security concerns, the company has said.
Anthropic said in a statement t... [2038 chars]

title: Trump made more than $1bn from crypto in first year back in office
description: The president's crypto income far outpaces his earnings from real estate and Trump-themed items such as watches.
content:
US President Donald Trump made more than $1bn (£750m) last year from business dealings in cryptocurrency, according to his mandatory financial report for 2025.
In a 927-page disclosure, he reported $635m in royalties from a Trump meme coin that has p... [3647 chars]

title: Afghan Taliban launch strikes on border with Pakistan as tensions escalate
description: Pakistan's military says it shot down four rudimentary drones and will respond to any further provocation.
content:
Afghanistan's Taliban regime said they have carried out strikes on targets along the border with Pakistan, injuring several people in Pakistan's southwestern Balochistan province.
Pakistan's military said it had shot down four rudimentary drones and ... [1592 chars]

title: The 29 Finalists for Astronomy Photographer of the Year
description: The Royal Observatory Greenwich and ZWO have unveiled the shortlisted photographs for their 18th annual Astronomy Photographer of the Year.
content: The Royal Observatory Greenwich has announced the shortlist for its annual ZWO Astronomy Photographer of the Year competition. This year’s edition received nearly 4,000 entries from dedicated amateur and professional photographers from 66 different c... [2227 chars]

title: France 3-0 Sweden: Les Bleus 'a level above' other World Cup teams with Kylian Mbappe 'on verge of greatness' says Gary Neville
description: France beat Sweden 3-0 in the round of 32; Gary Neville described it as a statement win with Les Bleus a level above any other team at the World Cup; Kylian Mbappe scored two goals, with the Sky Sports pundit praising the forward; France face Paraguay in the last 16 on Saturday
content:
Gary Neville says France are "a level above" any other team at the World Cup after they cruised to a 3-0 win against Sweden in the round of 32.
